summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic Tour core component (#265) in the ars-components crate, following the DOM-free architecture pattern established by Popover, with full test coverage, mutation validation, spec conformance, and a merged PR.
summary.outcome: Tour agnostic core was fully implemented and merged via PR with 28 unit tests, 2 spec-conformance tests, property-based invariant tests, 20 insta snapshots, 95.47% line coverage, and 0 mutation survivors out of 217 tested mutants.

repo: DOM-free architecture is a first-class constraint: cores emit typed Effect intents (e.g. FocusStepContent, ScrollTargetIntoView, MeasureSpotlight) and accept adapter feedback events (PositioningUpdate, SpotlightUpdate, SetZIndex); adapters own all live DOM work.
Component IDs follow the pattern ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id) producing root id, step-content id-step-content, title id-step-title, description id-step-description.
Lifecycle helpers follow Popover: open_plan, close_to_inactive_plan, complete_plan, step_change_plan, open_lifecycle_effects, close_lifecycle_effects.
on_props_changed must panic if id changes after init, and must sync controlled open, steps, and behavior props via SyncProps.
initial_effects must mirror active initial state from auto_start, default_open, or controlled open: Some(true).
PRs that add .snap files require the snapshot-reviewed label; issue closure flows through 'Closes #NNN' in the PR body, never manual close.
cargo xci-fast is the required pre-push gate; cargo xtask spec validate checks spec consistency; cargo mutants triages must result in 0 missed or justified .cargo/mutants.toml skips.
Post-implementation audit requires 3 phases with two consecutive clean 'anything else?' rounds before moving to coverage audit.
GitHub Project board status updates are done via GraphQL API (gh api graphql mutation updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue).
Worktrees are used for task isolation; the branch naming convention observed was t3code/<hash>.

code.finding: New Tour core module; implements State (Inactive, Active{step_index}, Completed), Event, Effect, StepType, Step, SpotlightRect, SpotlightSnapshot, Context, Props, Messages, Part, Machine, Api, ConnectApi without any DOM access.

Primary implementation pattern for all overlay agnostic cores; Tour mirrors its module structure: module docs, imports, effect enum, state/event/messages/context/props/part helpers, Machine, Api, tests.

Proptest invariants for overlay machines; Tour invariants added as ignored nightly tests covering state/context agreement, current_step bounds, closed-state feedback clearing, and out-of-range GoToStep no-ops.
Spec conformance tests check anatomy parts match spec; tour_anatomy_matches_spec() and StepIndicator{index} data-ars-step emission added.

workflow: TDD with failing tests first: write skeleton, run tests to get red, implement to green — avoids implementing without test coverage.
cargo mutants should be run after all other tests are green; first pass typically surfaces 30-40 survivors that require targeted test additions rather than broad skips.
cargo insta accept can be run crate-scoped; --unreferenced=reject exits 1 on pre-existing unrelated unreferenced snapshots so filter with -- overlay::tour for scoped verification.

Proptest on complex state machines catches real edge cases that unit tests miss (e.g., controlled-props leaving machine active with zero steps, active step list shrink misaligning State::Active and Context::current_step).
Spec drift should be fixed in the same PR as the implementation; adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) also need to be updated to match the new core API surface.
Mutation testing for a ~1500-line Rust state machine with 217 mutants takes 30-60 minutes; plan for multiple polling cycles.

friction: cargo mutants ran for ~60+ minutes across three passes requiring extensive polling; the first pass found 38 survivors, second 4, third 0 — three full reruns were needed.

cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject cannot be run cleanly at crate scope because pre-existing unrelated unreferenced snapshots outside Tour cause exit 1, making the required verification command effectively broken for Tour-only validation.
Dynamic step insertion had a real bug: updating Context::current_step without updating State::Active{step_index}, only caught by unit tests after initial compilation succeeded.

Proptest found a controlled-props edge case (active machine with zero steps after prop sync) that required two separate fixes: apply step changes before controlled open events, and close when active steps list becomes empty.

Adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) had multiple rounds of drift: first pass fixed ars_core::tour import, second pass fixed messages/locale incorrectly placed in core Props construction — required three audit rounds to reach two consecutive clean rounds.
The spec sample code still used platform focus/ID-based APIs, requiring manual spec surgery across tour.md, leptos tour.md, and dioxus tour.md in addition to the implementation work.
StrongSend type mismatch in the effect-run test harness caused an initial compile failure that required a targeted patch before any tests could run.
open_items: crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs has unstaged formatter churn that was intentionally excluded from this PR and needs a separate cleanup commit.
Pre-existing unreferenced snapshots outside Tour scope need to be cleaned up so cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject can pass cleanly at crate level.
Codex review loop (@codex review, poll for thumbs-up, address threads) is the next pending step after PR push — not shown as completed in the transcript.
Tour coverage at 95.47% lines / 96.49% regions leaves a small uncovered slice; specific uncovered branches were not identified and may warrant follow-up tests.
Adapter implementations (Leptos Tour component, Dioxus Tour component) are explicitly out of scope for #265 and will need separate issues/PRs.
